what_it_does: Provides a Markdown template with YAML frontmatter and prose section placeholders for creating a neutral, descriptive ledger record (a "terrain-entry") of a code or design substrate.
observations: YAML frontmatter defines a schema for a terrain-entry with fields such as type, register, aliases, tags, status, coverage, relation, kind, path, created, last_active, size, git_remote, secrets, and move_intent.
The body uses placeholder sections: What it is, How it works, What it connects to, When / where, and Coverage / evidence.
Status defaults to `unconfirmed` and is documented as a CONFIRMED-only field requiring explicit confirmation.
Typed relation fields (e.g., `relates-to`, `depends-on`, `derived-from`) are listed as examples with zero or more `[[alias]]` values.
The file contains no filled-in content; it is a skeleton with angle-bracket placeholders and inline comments.
standouts: Explicitly neutral, non-ranking ledger template with lifecycle signals like `status` and `move_intent`.
Rich typed-relation vocabulary in frontmatter to express graph edges beyond simple links.
Coverage counters embedded in metadata to track how much of the substrate has actually been read.
conventions: YAML frontmatter followed by Markdown body.
Placeholder values wrapped in angle brackets with explanatory comments.
Enum options written inline next to fields.
Vault links use `[[…]]` syntax in typed relation arrays.
concerns: All fields are empty placeholders, so consistency depends entirely on human discipline.
`status: unconfirmed` is the default but there is no visible workflow for confirmation.
`coverage.last_read` shows `2026-06-26`, which may be a future placeholder.
`aliases: ["<name>"]` is not a valid vault link target until replaced.
`relation` and `kind` are left blank with only enum comments, risking inconsistent values.
notes: A blank, schema-driven ledger template for documenting one code or design substrate with metadata, coverage, and typed relations.
questions: What is the full controlled vocabulary for `register` and `relation`?
How is the `coverage` block updated—manually or by tooling?
What does `terrain-entry` specifically denote in this ledger ontology?
Who or what confirms an entry to move `status` out of `unconfirmed`?
Are the typed relation keys fixed, or can new edge kinds be added?
What tooling consumes these templates and renders the graph?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to serve as a template for a ledger entry, and the file is exactly that—a reusable skeleton with placeholders and schema hints.
intention_signals: To standardize how each codebase or design artifact is recorded in a vault, separating descriptive metadata from interpretive prose while tracking confirmation status, read coverage, movement intent, and relational context.
novelty: Combines a frontmatter schema with explicit coverage counters, a lifecycle/movement intent field, and a small vocabulary of typed relations, all framed as a neutral, non-ranking documentation substrate.
contribution: A reusable, structured entry template that enforces consistent metadata, coverage tracking, and typed relational links for ledger records.
summary_for_embedding: A Markdown template with YAML frontmatter and section placeholders for creating a neutral, descriptive terrain-entry ledger record of a code or design substrate.
intention_for_embedding: To standardize documentation of individual codebase or design artifacts in an orienteering ledger with metadata, coverage, and typed relations.
